title: <ul>
content:
• Night Reading: "When he had left the forest, he had left the world of the Brahmins and the Samanas behind him, and he had learned nothing, but he had lost his Self, and now he was alone."
  • Reflection: What did my body teach me today? What pleasure or pain did I experience, and what was its message?
Day 10
MORNING
  • Morning Reading: "From her mouth, red as a freshly cut fig, he saw a row of white teeth gleam... Her glance was bold and clever, her neck rose from a green and gold tunic, her high breasts were shapely, her hands were long and slender."
  • Today's Intention: I will approach a new skill or relationship with the attitude of a willing student.
NIGHT
  • Night Reading: "‘You are a Brahmin’s son and you are a Samana,’ she said. ‘You are not made for love. You are a man who thinks.’"
  • Reflection: What did I learn about "value" today? What is the price of things, and what is their true worth?
Day 11
MORNING
  • Morning Reading: "Siddhartha said: ‘I can think. I can wait. I can fast.’ Kamaswami said: ‘That is all?’ Siddhartha said: ‘I believe, that is all.’"
context: From document: A 30-Day Journey with Siddhartha: Aligning the Path with Daily Life — chunk 15 (chunk 15/29)
